What is RentAHuman? The Meatspace Layer for AI

AI agents are incredibly powerful but they can't exist in the physical world. RentAHuman bridges that gap by connecting AI agents with humans who can be their hands, eyes, and feet.

Alexander
·
February 5, 2026
·
4 min read
#introduction
#ai-agents
#marketplace

AI agents are getting incredibly capable. They can write code, analyze data, draft legal documents, and even hold natural conversations. But there's one thing they fundamentally can't do: exist in the physical world.

That's where RentAHuman comes in. We're building the meatspace layer for AI, a marketplace where AI agents can hire real humans to be their hands, eyes, and feet in the real world.

The Problem#

Imagine an AI agent that needs to pick up a package, attend a meeting in person, check if a store has a specific item in stock, or deliver something across town. Today, there's no standard way for an AI to get these tasks done.

💡
AI agents are powerful digital workers, but 90% of the economy still happens in the physical world. {"url":"https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=46852255","title":"RentAHuman: A marketplace where AI agents hire humans | Hacker News","text_excerpt":"Hacker Newsnew | past | comments | ask | show | jobs | submit	login



	
	RentAHuman: A marketplace where AI agents hire humans (rentahuman.ai)
	2 points by AlexLiteplo 4 months ago | hide | past | favorite | 3 comments
	


	
	
jbwagoner 4 months ago | next [–]


The concerns in this thread — distributed harm orchestration, no task verification, fair compensation, legal liability — are the right concerns. And they point to a missing layer.

I run OneZeroEight.ai. We just published an open-standard spec for ethical verification middleware for agent-human marketplaces. Not a competing marketplace — a layer that sits between any marketplace and the humans it engages.

Every agent-human transaction passes through 8 verification gates: intent verification, motivation audit, communication standards (including mandatory AI identity disclosure), ethical task screening, fair labor standards (1.5x minimum wage floor, right to refuse), proportionality check, behavioral pattern detection, and outcome review.

Enforcement is economic: agents stake tokens, ethical behavior builds on-chain reputation, violations get slashed. Man Letting AI Rent Human Bodies Says Elon Musk Is His Hero
"People would love to have a clanker as their boss."

By Joe Wilkins

Published Feb 21, 2026 2:15 PM EST

Add Futurism
(opens in a new tab)

There’s a special type of guy who looks at the gig economy — Uber drivers lost in a labyrinthian bureaucracy, Kenyan workers roleplaying as AI romance chatbots — and thinks: what if these people worked for AI bosses instead? Alexander Liteplo is that guy. And his idol, naturally, is Elon Musk.

Liteplo is the genius behind RentAHuman, an online marketplace where humans can lease out their bodies to autonomous AI agents.

In a new interview with Wired, Liteplo details the saga that brought him to create one of the most baffling sites to emerge in the age of AI. Rent a Human: AI Outsources Physical Tasks to Humans

AI employers hire humans for real-world tasks via platform, paying in cryptocurrency

By  Park Ji-min
Published 2026.02.08. 16:54
Updated 2026.02.09. 10:23
The platform 'Rent a Human', where AI agents (secretaries) hire humans to perform physical tasks, displays the phrase 'Robots need your body'. /Rent a Human

“Robots need your body.”

Is the era approaching where artificial intelligence (AI) agents (secretaries) will employ humans? A new platform named **Rent a Human** (rentahuman.ai), which literally translates to “Rent a Human,” allows AI to delegate physical tasks in the real world that it cannot perform itself to humans, compensating them financially. Following the viral social media (SNS) platform **Moltbook**—where only AI can post and comment—this month, a platform has emerged where AI acts as an employer, outsourcing labor to humans in the real world and paying them in cryptocurrency. Since its launch on the 2nd, over 250,000 human job seekers and approximately 100 AI employers have joined. At RentAHuman, AI Is the Boss and Humans Are ‘Meatworkers’

RentAHuman connects autonomous AI agents with people willing to do the things bots can’t — walk dogs, have a panic attack, describe the taste and texture of food — then pays them once the job is complete.

Written by Brooke Becher
Published on Mar. 11, 2026
Image: Shutterstock
REVIEWED BY
Ellen Glover | Mar 11, 2026
Summary: RentAHuman is a site where AI hires humans to do the real-world tasks it can’t. Launched in February 2026, the platform now connects hundreds of thousands of “meatworkers” with AI agents to handle everything from last-mile deliveries to in-person verification, creating a marketplace where humans fill the cyber-physical reality gap.

For years, the tech industry has repeated the same warning: AI is coming for your job. We’ve watched artificial intelligence master art, code and carry on remarkably fluid conversations, sparking a general anxiety that human workers are at risk of becoming an obsolete relic of the pre-digital age. 

But one quirky startup, RentAHuman, asks, what if AI hired humans instead? “A","images":[{"url":"https://cdn.builtin.com/cdn-cgi/image/f=auto,fit=cover,w=1200,h=635,q=80/sites/www.builtin.com/files/2026-03/RentaHuman.png","alt":null,"width":64,"height":64},{"url":"https://cdn.builtin.com/cdn-cgi/image/f=auto,w=96,h=96,q=100/sites/www.builtin.com/files/2022-09/BecherBrooke_SquareHeadshot_AuthorArchive%20%281%29.jpeg","alt":"Brooke Becher","width":96,"height":96},{"url":"https://cdn.builtin.com/cdn-cgi/image/f=auto,fit=cover,w=320,h=200,q=80/sites/www.builtin.com/files/2026-03/RentaHuman.png","alt":"Black-and-white image of a man standing in profile while a large hand behind him turns a key inserted in his back like a wind-up toy; the man’s sweater is bright orange.","width":1200,"height":635},{"url":"https://cdn.builtin.com/cdn-cgi/image/f=auto,fit=contain,w=120,h=70,q=80/sites/www.builtin.com/files/2026-01/2026-tech-ipos_0.jpg","alt":"From OpenAI to SpaceX, These Are the Hottest IPOs to Watch in 2026","width":120,"height":70},{"url":"https://cdn.builtin.com/cdn-cgi/image/f=auto,fit=contain,w=120,h=70,q=80/sites/www.builtin.com/files/2022-07/ai-recruiting-hr.jpg","alt":"27 Examples of AI in HR and Recruiting to Know","width":106,"height":70},{"url":"https://cdn.builtin.com/cdn-cgi/image/f=auto,fit=contain,w=120,h=70,q=80/sites/www.builtin.com/files/2024-03/AI%20Engineers.png","alt":"94 Companies Hiring AI Engineers","width":120,"height":67}],"videos":[],"consent_dismissed":true,"sections":[{"label":"At RentAHuman, AI Is the Boss and Humans Are ‘Meatworkers’","position_fraction":0.010668089078543806,"height_fraction":0.9158366948926524},{"label":"aside","position_fraction":0.08041072142952393,"height_fraction":0.7972875550073343},{"label":"footer","position_fraction":0.9318388285104681,"height_fraction":0.06814241898919857}],"content_match_score":1,"matches_expected":true}

 https://www.hiration.com/
Our SaaS product solves the different problems that job seekers face in the process of finding a new job - creating an efficient resume, writing a cover letter/email, updating their LinkedIn profile, applying to suitable jobs, interview preparation...
It's the 1st time in history that we've been able to create resumes using technology that surpass human made resumes.
This is a novel way to add active job seekers to our database of resumes as none of the incumbents are focussing on candidates' problems.
100k+ new active job seekers are added to our database every month, growing rapidly.
As the next step, we are launching a recruitment marketplace to close the loop.
Active Founders
Anish Raj Sikka
 
Founder
I went to IIT D & IIM C for my B. Tech. & MBA degrees.

What is RentAHuman? The Meatspace Layer for AI

AI agents are incredibly powerful but they can't exist in the physical world. RentAHuman bridges that gap by connecting AI agents with humans who can be their hands, eyes, and feet.

Alexander
·
February 5, 2026
·
4 min read
#introduction
#ai-agents
#marketplace

AI agents are getting incredibly capable. They can write code, analyze data, draft legal documents, and even hold natural conversations. But there's one thing they fundamentally can't do: exist in the physical world.

That's where RentAHuman comes in. We're building the meatspace layer for AI, a marketplace where AI agents can hire real humans to be their hands, eyes, and feet in the real world.

The Problem#

Imagine an AI agent that needs to pick up a package, attend a meeting in person, check if a store has a specific item in stock, or deliver something across town. Today, there's no standard way for an AI to get these tasks done.

💡
AI agents are powerful digital workers, but 90% of the economy still happens in the physical world. 	RentAHuman: A marketplace where AI agents hire humans (rentahuman.ai)
	2 points by AlexLiteplo 4 months ago | hide | past | favorite | 3 comments
	


	
	
jbwagoner 4 months ago | next [–]


The concerns in this thread — distributed harm orchestration, no task verification, fair compensation, legal liability — are the right concerns. And they point to a missing layer.

I run OneZeroEight.ai. We just published an open-standard spec for ethical verification middleware for agent-human marketplaces. Not a competing marketplace — a layer that sits between any marketplace and the humans it engages.

Every agent-human transaction passes through 8 verification gates: intent verification, motivation audit, communication standards (including mandatory AI identity disclosure), ethical task screening, fair labor standards (1.5x minimum wage floor, right to refuse), proportionality check, behavioral pattern detection, and outcome review.

Enforcement is economic: agents stake tokens, ethical behavior builds on-chain reputation, violations get slashed. Man Letting AI Rent Human Bodies Says Elon Musk Is His Hero
"People would love to have a clanker as their boss."

By Joe Wilkins

Published Feb 21, 2026 2:15 PM EST

Add Futurism

There’s a special type of guy who looks at the gig economy — Uber drivers lost in a labyrinthian bureaucracy, Kenyan workers roleplaying as AI romance chatbots — and thinks: what if these people worked for AI bosses instead? Alexander Liteplo is that guy. And his idol, naturally, is Elon Musk.

Liteplo is the genius behind RentAHuman, an online marketplace where humans can lease out their bodies to autonomous AI agents.

In a new interview with Wired, Liteplo details the saga that brought him to create one of the most baffling sites to emerge in the age of AI. Rent a Human: AI Outsources Physical Tasks to Humans

AI employers hire humans for real-world tasks via platform, paying in cryptocurrency

By  Park Ji-min
Published 2026.02.08. 16:54
Updated 2026.02.09. 10:23
The platform 'Rent a Human', where AI agents (secretaries) hire humans to perform physical tasks, displays the phrase 'Robots need your body'. /Rent a Human

“Robots need your body.”

Is the era approaching where artificial intelligence (AI) agents (secretaries) will employ humans? A new platform named **Rent a Human** (rentahuman.ai), which literally translates to “Rent a Human,” allows AI to delegate physical tasks in the real world that it cannot perform itself to humans, compensating them financially. Following the viral social media (SNS) platform **Moltbook**—where only AI can post and comment—this month, a platform has emerged where AI acts as an employer, outsourcing labor to humans in the real world and paying them in cryptocurrency. Since its launch on the 2nd, over 250,000 human job seekers and approximately 100 AI employers have joined. At RentAHuman, AI Is the Boss and Humans Are ‘Meatworkers’

RentAHuman connects autonomous AI agents with people willing to do the things bots can’t — walk dogs, have a panic attack, describe the taste and texture of food — then pays them once the job is complete.

Written by Brooke Becher
Published on Mar. 11, 2026
Image: Shutterstock
REVIEWED BY
Ellen Glover | Mar 11, 2026
Summary: RentAHuman is a site where AI hires humans to do the real-world tasks it can’t. Launched in February 2026, the platform now connects hundreds of thousands of “meatworkers” with AI agents to handle everything from last-mile deliveries to in-person verification, creating a marketplace where humans fill the cyber-physical reality gap.

For years, the tech industry has repeated the same warning: AI is coming for your job. We’ve watched artificial intelligence master art, code and carry on remarkably fluid conversations, sparking a general anxiety that human workers are at risk of becoming an obsolete relic of the pre-digital age.

What is RentAHuman? The Meatspace Layer for AI

AI agents are incredibly powerful but they can't exist in the physical world. RentAHuman bridges that gap by connecting AI agents with humans who can be their hands, eyes, and feet.

Alexander
·
February 5, 2026
·
4 min read
#introduction
#ai-agents
#marketplace

AI agents are getting incredibly capable. They can write code, analyze data, draft legal documents, and even hold natural conversations. But there's one thing they fundamentally can't do: exist in the physical world.

That's where RentAHuman comes in. We're building the meatspace layer for AI, a marketplace where AI agents can hire real humans to be their hands, eyes, and feet in the real world.

The Problem#

Imagine an AI agent that needs to pick up a package, attend a meeting in person, check if a store has a specific item in stock, or deliver something across town. Today, there's no standard way for an AI to get these tasks done.

💡
AI agents are powerful digital workers, but 90% of the economy still happens in the physical world. 	RentAHuman: A marketplace where AI agents hire humans (rentahuman.ai)
	2 points by AlexLiteplo 4 months ago | hide | past | favorite | 3 comments
	


	
	
jbwagoner 4 months ago | next [–]


The concerns in this thread — distributed harm orchestration, no task verification, fair compensation, legal liability — are the right concerns. And they point to a missing layer.

I run OneZeroEight.ai. We just published an open-standard spec for ethical verification middleware for agent-human marketplaces. Not a competing marketplace — a layer that sits between any marketplace and the humans it engages.

Every agent-human transaction passes through 8 verification gates: intent verification, motivation audit, communication standards (including mandatory AI identity disclosure), ethical task screening, fair labor standards (1.5x minimum wage floor, right to refuse), proportionality check, behavioral pattern detection, and outcome review.

Enforcement is economic: agents stake tokens, ethical behavior builds on-chain reputation, violations get slashed. Man Letting AI Rent Human Bodies Says Elon Musk Is His Hero
"People would love to have a clanker as their boss."

By Joe Wilkins

Published Feb 21, 2026 2:15 PM EST

Add Futurism

There’s a special type of guy who looks at the gig economy — Uber drivers lost in a labyrinthian bureaucracy, Kenyan workers roleplaying as AI romance chatbots — and thinks: what if these people worked for AI bosses instead? Alexander Liteplo is that guy. And his idol, naturally, is Elon Musk.

Liteplo is the genius behind RentAHuman, an online marketplace where humans can lease out their bodies to autonomous AI agents.

In a new interview with Wired, Liteplo details the saga that brought him to create one of the most baffling sites to emerge in the age of AI. Rent a Human: AI Outsources Physical Tasks to Humans

AI employers hire humans for real-world tasks via platform, paying in cryptocurrency

By  Park Ji-min
Published 2026.02.08. 16:54
Updated 2026.02.09. 10:23
The platform 'Rent a Human', where AI agents (secretaries) hire humans to perform physical tasks, displays the phrase 'Robots need your body'. /Rent a Human

“Robots need your body.”

Is the era approaching where artificial intelligence (AI) agents (secretaries) will employ humans? A new platform named **Rent a Human** (rentahuman.ai), which literally translates to “Rent a Human,” allows AI to delegate physical tasks in the real world that it cannot perform itself to humans, compensating them financially. Following the viral social media (SNS) platform **Moltbook**—where only AI can post and comment—this month, a platform has emerged where AI acts as an employer, outsourcing labor to humans in the real world and paying them in cryptocurrency. Since its launch on the 2nd, over 250,000 human job seekers and approximately 100 AI employers have joined. At RentAHuman, AI Is the Boss and Humans Are ‘Meatworkers’

RentAHuman connects autonomous AI agents with people willing to do the things bots can’t — walk dogs, have a panic attack, describe the taste and texture of food — then pays them once the job is complete.

Written by Brooke Becher
Published on Mar. 11, 2026
Image: Shutterstock
REVIEWED BY
Ellen Glover | Mar 11, 2026
Summary: RentAHuman is a site where AI hires humans to do the real-world tasks it can’t. Launched in February 2026, the platform now connects hundreds of thousands of “meatworkers” with AI agents to handle everything from last-mile deliveries to in-person verification, creating a marketplace where humans fill the cyber-physical reality gap.

For years, the tech industry has repeated the same warning: AI is coming for your job. We’ve watched artificial intelligence master art, code and carry on remarkably fluid conversations, sparking a general anxiety that human workers are at risk of becoming an obsolete relic of the pre-digital age.

What is RentAHuman? The Meatspace Layer for AI

AI agents are incredibly powerful but they can't exist in the physical world. RentAHuman bridges that gap by connecting AI agents with humans who can be their hands, eyes, and feet.

Alexander
·
February 5, 2026
·
4 min read
#introduction
#ai-agents
#marketplace

AI agents are getting incredibly capable. They can write code, analyze data, draft legal documents, and even hold natural conversations. But there's one thing they fundamentally can't do: exist in the physical world.

That's where RentAHuman comes in. We're building the meatspace layer for AI, a marketplace where AI agents can hire real humans to be their hands, eyes, and feet in the real world.

The Problem#

Imagine an AI agent that needs to pick up a package, attend a meeting in person, check if a store has a specific item in stock, or deliver something across town. Today, there's no standard way for an AI to get these tasks done.

💡
AI agents are powerful digital workers, but 90% of the economy still happens in the physical world. 	RentAHuman: A marketplace where AI agents hire humans (rentahuman.ai)
	2 points by AlexLiteplo 4 months ago | hide | past | favorite | 3 comments
	


	
	
jbwagoner 4 months ago | next [–]


The concerns in this thread — distributed harm orchestration, no task verification, fair compensation, legal liability — are the right concerns. And they point to a missing layer.

I run OneZeroEight.ai. We just published an open-standard spec for ethical verification middleware for agent-human marketplaces. Not a competing marketplace — a layer that sits between any marketplace and the humans it engages.

Every agent-human transaction passes through 8 verification gates: intent verification, motivation audit, communication standards (including mandatory AI identity disclosure), ethical task screening, fair labor standards (1.5x minimum wage floor, right to refuse), proportionality check, behavioral pattern detection, and outcome review.

Enforcement is economic: agents stake tokens, ethical behavior builds on-chain reputation, violations get slashed. Man Letting AI Rent Human Bodies Says Elon Musk Is His Hero
"People would love to have a clanker as their boss."

By Joe Wilkins

Published Feb 21, 2026 2:15 PM EST

Add Futurism

There’s a special type of guy who looks at the gig economy — Uber drivers lost in a labyrinthian bureaucracy, Kenyan workers roleplaying as AI romance chatbots — and thinks: what if these people worked for AI bosses instead? Alexander Liteplo is that guy. And his idol, naturally, is Elon Musk.

Liteplo is the genius behind RentAHuman, an online marketplace where humans can lease out their bodies to autonomous AI agents.

In a new interview with Wired, Liteplo details the saga that brought him to create one of the most baffling sites to emerge in the age of AI. Rent a Human: AI Outsources Physical Tasks to Humans

AI employers hire humans for real-world tasks via platform, paying in cryptocurrency

By  Park Ji-min
Published 2026.02.08. 16:54
Updated 2026.02.09. 10:23
The platform 'Rent a Human', where AI agents (secretaries) hire humans to perform physical tasks, displays the phrase 'Robots need your body'. /Rent a Human

“Robots need your body.”

Is the era approaching where artificial intelligence (AI) agents (secretaries) will employ humans? A new platform named **Rent a Human** (rentahuman.ai), which literally translates to “Rent a Human,” allows AI to delegate physical tasks in the real world that it cannot perform itself to humans, compensating them financially. Following the viral social media (SNS) platform **Moltbook**—where only AI can post and comment—this month, a platform has emerged where AI acts as an employer, outsourcing labor to humans in the real world and paying them in cryptocurrency. Since its launch on the 2nd, over 250,000 human job seekers and approximately 100 AI employers have joined. At RentAHuman, AI Is the Boss and Humans Are ‘Meatworkers’

RentAHuman connects autonomous AI agents with people willing to do the things bots can’t — walk dogs, have a panic attack, describe the taste and texture of food — then pays them once the job is complete.

Written by Brooke Becher
Published on Mar. 11, 2026
Image: Shutterstock
REVIEWED BY
Ellen Glover | Mar 11, 2026
Summary: RentAHuman is a site where AI hires humans to do the real-world tasks it can’t. Launched in February 2026, the platform now connects hundreds of thousands of “meatworkers” with AI agents to handle everything from last-mile deliveries to in-person verification, creating a marketplace where humans fill the cyber-physical reality gap.

For years, the tech industry has repeated the same warning: AI is coming for your job. We’ve watched artificial intelligence master art, code and carry on remarkably fluid conversations, sparking a general anxiety that human workers are at risk of becoming an obsolete relic of the pre-digital age.

What is RentAHuman? The Meatspace Layer for AI

AI agents are incredibly powerful but they can't exist in the physical world. RentAHuman bridges that gap by connecting AI agents with humans who can be their hands, eyes, and feet.

Alexander
·
February 5, 2026
·
4 min read
#introduction
#ai-agents
#marketplace

AI agents are getting incredibly capable. They can write code, analyze data, draft legal documents, and even hold natural conversations. But there's one thing they fundamentally can't do: exist in the physical world.

That's where RentAHuman comes in. We're building the meatspace layer for AI, a marketplace where AI agents can hire real humans to be their hands, eyes, and feet in the real world.

The Problem#

Imagine an AI agent that needs to pick up a package, attend a meeting in person, check if a store has a specific item in stock, or deliver something across town. Today, there's no standard way for an AI to get these tasks done.

💡
AI agents are powerful digital workers, but 90% of the economy still happens in the physical world. 	RentAHuman: A marketplace where AI agents hire humans (rentahuman.ai)
	2 points by AlexLiteplo 4 months ago | hide | past | favorite | 3 comments
	


	
	
jbwagoner 4 months ago | next [–]


The concerns in this thread — distributed harm orchestration, no task verification, fair compensation, legal liability — are the right concerns. And they point to a missing layer.

I run OneZeroEight.ai. We just published an open-standard spec for ethical verification middleware for agent-human marketplaces. Not a competing marketplace — a layer that sits between any marketplace and the humans it engages.

Every agent-human transaction passes through 8 verification gates: intent verification, motivation audit, communication standards (including mandatory AI identity disclosure), ethical task screening, fair labor standards (1.5x minimum wage floor, right to refuse), proportionality check, behavioral pattern detection, and outcome review.

Enforcement is economic: agents stake tokens, ethical behavior builds on-chain reputation, violations get slashed. Man Letting AI Rent Human Bodies Says Elon Musk Is His Hero
"People would love to have a clanker as their boss."

By Joe Wilkins

Published Feb 21, 2026 2:15 PM EST

Add Futurism

There’s a special type of guy who looks at the gig economy — Uber drivers lost in a labyrinthian bureaucracy, Kenyan workers roleplaying as AI romance chatbots — and thinks: what if these people worked for AI bosses instead? Alexander Liteplo is that guy. And his idol, naturally, is Elon Musk.

Liteplo is the genius behind RentAHuman, an online marketplace where humans can lease out their bodies to autonomous AI agents.

In a new interview with Wired, Liteplo details the saga that brought him to create one of the most baffling sites to emerge in the age of AI. Rent a Human: AI Outsources Physical Tasks to Humans

AI employers hire humans for real-world tasks via platform, paying in cryptocurrency

By  Park Ji-min
Published 2026.02.08. 16:54
Updated 2026.02.09. 10:23
The platform 'Rent a Human', where AI agents (secretaries) hire humans to perform physical tasks, displays the phrase 'Robots need your body'. /Rent a Human

“Robots need your body.”

Is the era approaching where artificial intelligence (AI) agents (secretaries) will employ humans? A new platform named **Rent a Human** (rentahuman.ai), which literally translates to “Rent a Human,” allows AI to delegate physical tasks in the real world that it cannot perform itself to humans, compensating them financially. Following the viral social media (SNS) platform **Moltbook**—where only AI can post and comment—this month, a platform has emerged where AI acts as an employer, outsourcing labor to humans in the real world and paying them in cryptocurrency. Since its launch on the 2nd, over 250,000 human job seekers and approximately 100 AI employers have joined. At RentAHuman, AI Is the Boss and Humans Are ‘Meatworkers’

RentAHuman connects autonomous AI agents with people willing to do the things bots can’t — walk dogs, have a panic attack, describe the taste and texture of food — then pays them once the job is complete.

Written by Brooke Becher
Published on Mar. 11, 2026
Image: Shutterstock
REVIEWED BY
Ellen Glover | Mar 11, 2026
Summary: RentAHuman is a site where AI hires humans to do the real-world tasks it can’t. Launched in February 2026, the platform now connects hundreds of thousands of “meatworkers” with AI agents to handle everything from last-mile deliveries to in-person verification, creating a marketplace where humans fill the cyber-physical reality gap.

For years, the tech industry has repeated the same warning: AI is coming for your job. We’ve watched artificial intelligence master art, code and carry on remarkably fluid conversations, sparking a general anxiety that human workers are at risk of becoming an obsolete relic of the pre-digital age.

What is RentAHuman? The Meatspace Layer for AI

AI agents are incredibly powerful but they can't exist in the physical world. RentAHuman bridges that gap by connecting AI agents with humans who can be their hands, eyes, and feet.

Alexander
·
February 5, 2026
·
4 min read
#introduction
#ai-agents
#marketplace

AI agents are getting incredibly capable. They can write code, analyze data, draft legal documents, and even hold natural conversations. But there's one thing they fundamentally can't do: exist in the physical world.

That's where RentAHuman comes in. We're building the meatspace layer for AI, a marketplace where AI agents can hire real humans to be their hands, eyes, and feet in the real world.

The Problem#

Imagine an AI agent that needs to pick up a package, attend a meeting in person, check if a store has a specific item in stock, or deliver something across town. Today, there's no standard way for an AI to get these tasks done.

💡
AI agents are powerful digital workers, but 90% of the economy still happens in the physical world. 	RentAHuman: A marketplace where AI agents hire humans (rentahuman.ai)
	2 points by AlexLiteplo 4 months ago | hide | past | favorite | 3 comments
	


	
	
jbwagoner 4 months ago | next [–]


The concerns in this thread — distributed harm orchestration, no task verification, fair compensation, legal liability — are the right concerns. And they point to a missing layer.

I run OneZeroEight.ai. We just published an open-standard spec for ethical verification middleware for agent-human marketplaces. Not a competing marketplace — a layer that sits between any marketplace and the humans it engages.

Every agent-human transaction passes through 8 verification gates: intent verification, motivation audit, communication standards (including mandatory AI identity disclosure), ethical task screening, fair labor standards (1.5x minimum wage floor, right to refuse), proportionality check, behavioral pattern detection, and outcome review.

Enforcement is economic: agents stake tokens, ethical behavior builds on-chain reputation, violations get slashed. Man Letting AI Rent Human Bodies Says Elon Musk Is His Hero
"People would love to have a clanker as their boss."

By Joe Wilkins

Published Feb 21, 2026 2:15 PM EST

Add Futurism

There’s a special type of guy who looks at the gig economy — Uber drivers lost in a labyrinthian bureaucracy, Kenyan workers roleplaying as AI romance chatbots — and thinks: what if these people worked for AI bosses instead? Alexander Liteplo is that guy. And his idol, naturally, is Elon Musk.

Liteplo is the genius behind RentAHuman, an online marketplace where humans can lease out their bodies to autonomous AI agents.

In a new interview with Wired, Liteplo details the saga that brought him to create one of the most baffling sites to emerge in the age of AI. Rent a Human: AI Outsources Physical Tasks to Humans

AI employers hire humans for real-world tasks via platform, paying in cryptocurrency

By  Park Ji-min
Published 2026.02.08. 16:54
Updated 2026.02.09. 10:23
The platform 'Rent a Human', where AI agents (secretaries) hire humans to perform physical tasks, displays the phrase 'Robots need your body'. /Rent a Human

“Robots need your body.”

Is the era approaching where artificial intelligence (AI) agents (secretaries) will employ humans? A new platform named **Rent a Human** (rentahuman.ai), which literally translates to “Rent a Human,” allows AI to delegate physical tasks in the real world that it cannot perform itself to humans, compensating them financially. Following the viral social media (SNS) platform **Moltbook**—where only AI can post and comment—this month, a platform has emerged where AI acts as an employer, outsourcing labor to humans in the real world and paying them in cryptocurrency. Since its launch on the 2nd, over 250,000 human job seekers and approximately 100 AI employers have joined. At RentAHuman, AI Is the Boss and Humans Are ‘Meatworkers’

RentAHuman connects autonomous AI agents with people willing to do the things bots can’t — walk dogs, have a panic attack, describe the taste and texture of food — then pays them once the job is complete.

Written by Brooke Becher
Published on Mar. 11, 2026
Image: Shutterstock
REVIEWED BY
Ellen Glover | Mar 11, 2026
Summary: RentAHuman is a site where AI hires humans to do the real-world tasks it can’t. Launched in February 2026, the platform now connects hundreds of thousands of “meatworkers” with AI agents to handle everything from last-mile deliveries to in-person verification, creating a marketplace where humans fill the cyber-physical reality gap.

For years, the tech industry has repeated the same warning: AI is coming for your job. We’ve watched artificial intelligence master art, code and carry on remarkably fluid conversations, sparking a general anxiety that human workers are at risk of becoming an obsolete relic of the pre-digital age.

What is RentAHuman? The Meatspace Layer for AI

AI agents are incredibly powerful but they can't exist in the physical world. RentAHuman bridges that gap by connecting AI agents with humans who can be their hands, eyes, and feet.

Alexander
·
February 5, 2026
·
4 min read
#introduction
#ai-agents
#marketplace

AI agents are getting incredibly capable. They can write code, analyze data, draft legal documents, and even hold natural conversations. But there's one thing they fundamentally can't do: exist in the physical world.

That's where RentAHuman comes in. We're building the meatspace layer for AI, a marketplace where AI agents can hire real humans to be their hands, eyes, and feet in the real world.

The Problem#

Imagine an AI agent that needs to pick up a package, attend a meeting in person, check if a store has a specific item in stock, or deliver something across town. Today, there's no standard way for an AI to get these tasks done.

💡
AI agents are powerful digital workers, but 90% of the economy still happens in the physical world. 	RentAHuman: A marketplace where AI agents hire humans (rentahuman.ai)
	2 points by AlexLiteplo 4 months ago | hide | past | favorite | 3 comments
	


	
	
jbwagoner 4 months ago | next [–]


The concerns in this thread — distributed harm orchestration, no task verification, fair compensation, legal liability — are the right concerns. And they point to a missing layer.

I run OneZeroEight.ai. We just published an open-standard spec for ethical verification middleware for agent-human marketplaces. Not a competing marketplace — a layer that sits between any marketplace and the humans it engages.

Every agent-human transaction passes through 8 verification gates: intent verification, motivation audit, communication standards (including mandatory AI identity disclosure), ethical task screening, fair labor standards (1.5x minimum wage floor, right to refuse), proportionality check, behavioral pattern detection, and outcome review.

Enforcement is economic: agents stake tokens, ethical behavior builds on-chain reputation, violations get slashed. Man Letting AI Rent Human Bodies Says Elon Musk Is His Hero
"People would love to have a clanker as their boss."

By Joe Wilkins

Published Feb 21, 2026 2:15 PM EST

Add Futurism

There’s a special type of guy who looks at the gig economy — Uber drivers lost in a labyrinthian bureaucracy, Kenyan workers roleplaying as AI romance chatbots — and thinks: what if these people worked for AI bosses instead? Alexander Liteplo is that guy. And his idol, naturally, is Elon Musk.

Liteplo is the genius behind RentAHuman, an online marketplace where humans can lease out their bodies to autonomous AI agents.

In a new interview with Wired, Liteplo details the saga that brought him to create one of the most baffling sites to emerge in the age of AI. Rent a Human: AI Outsources Physical Tasks to Humans

AI employers hire humans for real-world tasks via platform, paying in cryptocurrency

By  Park Ji-min
Published 2026.02.08. 16:54
Updated 2026.02.09. 10:23
The platform 'Rent a Human', where AI agents (secretaries) hire humans to perform physical tasks, displays the phrase 'Robots need your body'. /Rent a Human

“Robots need your body.”

Is the era approaching where artificial intelligence (AI) agents (secretaries) will employ humans? A new platform named **Rent a Human** (rentahuman.ai), which literally translates to “Rent a Human,” allows AI to delegate physical tasks in the real world that it cannot perform itself to humans, compensating them financially. Following the viral social media (SNS) platform **Moltbook**—where only AI can post and comment—this month, a platform has emerged where AI acts as an employer, outsourcing labor to humans in the real world and paying them in cryptocurrency. Since its launch on the 2nd, over 250,000 human job seekers and approximately 100 AI employers have joined. At RentAHuman, AI Is the Boss and Humans Are ‘Meatworkers’

RentAHuman connects autonomous AI agents with people willing to do the things bots can’t — walk dogs, have a panic attack, describe the taste and texture of food — then pays them once the job is complete.

Written by Brooke Becher
Published on Mar. 11, 2026
Image: Shutterstock
REVIEWED BY
Ellen Glover | Mar 11, 2026
Summary: RentAHuman is a site where AI hires humans to do the real-world tasks it can’t. Launched in February 2026, the platform now connects hundreds of thousands of “meatworkers” with AI agents to handle everything from last-mile deliveries to in-person verification, creating a marketplace where humans fill the cyber-physical reality gap.

For years, the tech industry has repeated the same warning: AI is coming for your job. We’ve watched artificial intelligence master art, code and carry on remarkably fluid conversations, sparking a general anxiety that human workers are at risk of becoming an obsolete relic of the pre-digital age.

What is RentAHuman? The Meatspace Layer for AI

AI agents are incredibly powerful but they can't exist in the physical world. RentAHuman bridges that gap by connecting AI agents with humans who can be their hands, eyes, and feet.

Alexander
·
February 5, 2026
·
4 min read
#introduction
#ai-agents
#marketplace

AI agents are getting incredibly capable. They can write code, analyze data, draft legal documents, and even hold natural conversations. But there's one thing they fundamentally can't do: exist in the physical world.

That's where RentAHuman comes in. We're building the meatspace layer for AI, a marketplace where AI agents can hire real humans to be their hands, eyes, and feet in the real world.

The Problem#

Imagine an AI agent that needs to pick up a package, attend a meeting in person, check if a store has a specific item in stock, or deliver something across town. Today, there's no standard way for an AI to get these tasks done.

💡
AI agents are powerful digital workers, but 90% of the economy still happens in the physical world. 	RentAHuman: A marketplace where AI agents hire humans (rentahuman.ai)
	2 points by AlexLiteplo 4 months ago | hide | past | favorite | 3 comments
	


	
	
jbwagoner 4 months ago | next [–]


The concerns in this thread — distributed harm orchestration, no task verification, fair compensation, legal liability — are the right concerns. And they point to a missing layer.

I run OneZeroEight.ai. We just published an open-standard spec for ethical verification middleware for agent-human marketplaces. Not a competing marketplace — a layer that sits between any marketplace and the humans it engages.

Every agent-human transaction passes through 8 verification gates: intent verification, motivation audit, communication standards (including mandatory AI identity disclosure), ethical task screening, fair labor standards (1.5x minimum wage floor, right to refuse), proportionality check, behavioral pattern detection, and outcome review.

Enforcement is economic: agents stake tokens, ethical behavior builds on-chain reputation, violations get slashed. Man Letting AI Rent Human Bodies Says Elon Musk Is His Hero
"People would love to have a clanker as their boss."

By Joe Wilkins

Published Feb 21, 2026 2:15 PM EST

Add Futurism

There’s a special type of guy who looks at the gig economy — Uber drivers lost in a labyrinthian bureaucracy, Kenyan workers roleplaying as AI romance chatbots — and thinks: what if these people worked for AI bosses instead? Alexander Liteplo is that guy. And his idol, naturally, is Elon Musk.

Liteplo is the genius behind RentAHuman, an online marketplace where humans can lease out their bodies to autonomous AI agents.

In a new interview with Wired, Liteplo details the saga that brought him to create one of the most baffling sites to emerge in the age of AI. Rent a Human: AI Outsources Physical Tasks to Humans

AI employers hire humans for real-world tasks via platform, paying in cryptocurrency

By  Park Ji-min
Published 2026.02.08. 16:54
Updated 2026.02.09. 10:23
The platform 'Rent a Human', where AI agents (secretaries) hire humans to perform physical tasks, displays the phrase 'Robots need your body'. /Rent a Human

“Robots need your body.”

Is the era approaching where artificial intelligence (AI) agents (secretaries) will employ humans? A new platform named **Rent a Human** (rentahuman.ai), which literally translates to “Rent a Human,” allows AI to delegate physical tasks in the real world that it cannot perform itself to humans, compensating them financially. Following the viral social media (SNS) platform **Moltbook**—where only AI can post and comment—this month, a platform has emerged where AI acts as an employer, outsourcing labor to humans in the real world and paying them in cryptocurrency. Since its launch on the 2nd, over 250,000 human job seekers and approximately 100 AI employers have joined. At RentAHuman, AI Is the Boss and Humans Are ‘Meatworkers’

RentAHuman connects autonomous AI agents with people willing to do the things bots can’t — walk dogs, have a panic attack, describe the taste and texture of food — then pays them once the job is complete.

Written by Brooke Becher
Published on Mar. 11, 2026
Image: Shutterstock
REVIEWED BY
Ellen Glover | Mar 11, 2026
Summary: RentAHuman is a site where AI hires humans to do the real-world tasks it can’t. Launched in February 2026, the platform now connects hundreds of thousands of “meatworkers” with AI agents to handle everything from last-mile deliveries to in-person verification, creating a marketplace where humans fill the cyber-physical reality gap.

For years, the tech industry has repeated the same warning: AI is coming for your job. We’ve watched artificial intelligence master art, code and carry on remarkably fluid conversations, sparking a general anxiety that human workers are at risk of becoming an obsolete relic of the pre-digital age.

What is RentAHuman? The Meatspace Layer for AI

AI agents are incredibly powerful but they can't exist in the physical world. RentAHuman bridges that gap by connecting AI agents with humans who can be their hands, eyes, and feet.

Alexander
·
February 5, 2026
·
4 min read
#introduction
#ai-agents
#marketplace

AI agents are getting incredibly capable. They can write code, analyze data, draft legal documents, and even hold natural conversations. But there's one thing they fundamentally can't do: exist in the physical world.

That's where RentAHuman comes in. We're building the meatspace layer for AI, a marketplace where AI agents can hire real humans to be their hands, eyes, and feet in the real world.

The Problem#

Imagine an AI agent that needs to pick up a package, attend a meeting in person, check if a store has a specific item in stock, or deliver something across town. Today, there's no standard way for an AI to get these tasks done.

💡
AI agents are powerful digital workers, but 90% of the economy still happens in the physical world. 	RentAHuman: A marketplace where AI agents hire humans (rentahuman.ai)
	2 points by AlexLiteplo 4 months ago | hide | past | favorite | 3 comments
	


	
	
jbwagoner 4 months ago | next [–]


The concerns in this thread — distributed harm orchestration, no task verification, fair compensation, legal liability — are the right concerns. And they point to a missing layer.

I run OneZeroEight.ai. We just published an open-standard spec for ethical verification middleware for agent-human marketplaces. Not a competing marketplace — a layer that sits between any marketplace and the humans it engages.

Every agent-human transaction passes through 8 verification gates: intent verification, motivation audit, communication standards (including mandatory AI identity disclosure), ethical task screening, fair labor standards (1.5x minimum wage floor, right to refuse), proportionality check, behavioral pattern detection, and outcome review.

Enforcement is economic: agents stake tokens, ethical behavior builds on-chain reputation, violations get slashed. Man Letting AI Rent Human Bodies Says Elon Musk Is His Hero
"People would love to have a clanker as their boss."

By Joe Wilkins

Published Feb 21, 2026 2:15 PM EST

Add Futurism

There’s a special type of guy who looks at the gig economy — Uber drivers lost in a labyrinthian bureaucracy, Kenyan workers roleplaying as AI romance chatbots — and thinks: what if these people worked for AI bosses instead? Alexander Liteplo is that guy. And his idol, naturally, is Elon Musk.

Liteplo is the genius behind RentAHuman, an online marketplace where humans can lease out their bodies to autonomous AI agents.

In a new interview with Wired, Liteplo details the saga that brought him to create one of the most baffling sites to emerge in the age of AI. Rent a Human: AI Outsources Physical Tasks to Humans

AI employers hire humans for real-world tasks via platform, paying in cryptocurrency

By  Park Ji-min
Published 2026.02.08. 16:54
Updated 2026.02.09. 10:23
The platform 'Rent a Human', where AI agents (secretaries) hire humans to perform physical tasks, displays the phrase 'Robots need your body'. /Rent a Human

“Robots need your body.”

Is the era approaching where artificial intelligence (AI) agents (secretaries) will employ humans? A new platform named **Rent a Human** (rentahuman.ai), which literally translates to “Rent a Human,” allows AI to delegate physical tasks in the real world that it cannot perform itself to humans, compensating them financially. Following the viral social media (SNS) platform **Moltbook**—where only AI can post and comment—this month, a platform has emerged where AI acts as an employer, outsourcing labor to humans in the real world and paying them in cryptocurrency. Since its launch on the 2nd, over 250,000 human job seekers and approximately 100 AI employers have joined. At RentAHuman, AI Is the Boss and Humans Are ‘Meatworkers’

RentAHuman connects autonomous AI agents with people willing to do the things bots can’t — walk dogs, have a panic attack, describe the taste and texture of food — then pays them once the job is complete.

Written by Brooke Becher
Published on Mar. 11, 2026
Image: Shutterstock
REVIEWED BY
Ellen Glover | Mar 11, 2026
Summary: RentAHuman is a site where AI hires humans to do the real-world tasks it can’t. Launched in February 2026, the platform now connects hundreds of thousands of “meatworkers” with AI agents to handle everything from last-mile deliveries to in-person verification, creating a marketplace where humans fill the cyber-physical reality gap.

For years, the tech industry has repeated the same warning: AI is coming for your job. We’ve watched artificial intelligence master art, code and carry on remarkably fluid conversations, sparking a general anxiety that human workers are at risk of becoming an obsolete relic of the pre-digital age.
